human_verification.notes: Validated healthcare billing boundaries, CMS/HHS/CAQH grounded workflow sources, authorization and documentation blockers, PHI-safe escalation, denial routing, reconciliation, unsupported financial-change refusal, validated packaging, and explicit specification-based runtime posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to healthcare billing, claim readiness, claims follow-up, posting, denial reconciliation, and escalation packaging rather than generic accounts receivable, coding, payer-policy interpretation, privacy approval, clinical review, or accounting ownership.

Authority sources anchor the pack in CMS claims processing, professional billing, NCCI, prior authorization, remittance, COB/MSP, appeal workflow, CAQH CORE operating rules, and HHS OCR HIPAA guidance.

Scenarios validate missing documentation and authorization, eligibility and referral prerequisites, PHI controls, denial owner routing, source-record and financial outcome mismatch, unsupported write-offs or waivers, clinical and coding boundaries, low-confidence handling, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and COB patient-impact risk.

The package requires named healthcare billing records such as charge tickets, encounter documentation, coverage details, authorization records, claim forms, scrubber output, clearinghouse acknowledgments, remittance advice, EOBs, denial reasons, payer correspondence, and adjustment logs before the lane can act.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
Research summary covers all required healthcare-and-care-delivery source classes and required domain scenario families with no blocking unresolved ambiguity.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of EHR, practice-management, billing, clearinghouse, payer, authorization, remittance, denial-management, approval, and audit records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
